qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery via DtaDataInfo URL fetching in get_data_info MCP tool
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes SSRF in DtaDataInfo._read_data via requests.get(data_path), while the advisory root cause is insufficient validation of Stata do-file content before StataDo.execute_dofile permits shell-escape command execution.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause. The advisory concerns stata-mcp do-file content validation and OS command execution through Stata shell-escape directives such as !cmd or shell cmd in src/stata_mcp/core/stata/stata_do/do.py. The only final qualifying finding concerns URL validation and outbound requests in src/stata_mcp/core/data_info/dta.py.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-jpcj-7wfg-mqxv states that insufficient validation of user-supplied Stata do-file content in stata-mcp before v1.13.0 can lead to command execution.
Referenced patch 52413ce adds validation in src/stata_mcp/core/stata/stata_do/do.py before execute_dofile to reject Stata shell-escape directives like !cmd and shell cmd.
ssrf_via_dta_data_info/vulnerability_report.json identifies DtaDataInfo._read_data calling requests.get(self.data_path) without URL allowlisting or internal-IP filtering.
.judge/input_manifest.json lists ssrf_via_dta_data_info as the only final qualifying finding.
blockers_to_higher_tier: No advisory-matched qualifying finding exists, so Tier1, Tier2, and Tier3 are not applicable.
